observe_decisions.object_type_reasoning: The image likely contains histopathological structures such as glandular architectures, which are common in pathology images examining tissues from the colon.
observe_decisions.color_mode_reasoning: The image is RGB, and color differences in H&E staining can carry important diagnostic information regarding tissue and cellular structures.
observe_decisions.filtration_reasoning: Sublevel filtration is suitable for capturing dark-on-light structures typical in stained tissue images where cellular and glandular boundaries are darker.

ph_signals.name: h1_dominant
ph_signals.description: H1 features significantly outnumber H0 — loop/cavity structure dominates
ph_signals.reasoning: When H1 features greatly exceed H0, the image has rich loop/cavity structure. Persistence landscapes capture multi-level H1 information via layered envelope functions, better than single-summary descriptors.

observe_decisions.object_type_reasoning: The image likely contains glandular structures, which are common in histopathology images, especially when assessing conditions like adenocarcinoma and adenoma.
observe_decisions.color_mode_reasoning: Color in histopathology images is diagnostically meaningful due to H&E staining, so using per-channel mode allows for capturing color-specific features.
observe_decisions.filtration_reasoning: Sublevel filtration is suitable for histopathology images where dark-stained regions (like cellular structures) need to be captured first in the filtration process.

ph_signals.name: rich_topology
ph_signals.description: Very many PH features with H1-heavy profile — multi-scale structure
ph_signals.reasoning: Extremely rich PH diagrams (>20K features) with H1 emphasis contain multi-scale topological information. Persistence landscapes' layered representation captures different scales via different landscape levels.

observe_decisions.object_type_reasoning: The image contains glandular structures typical of histopathology images, which are crucial for differentiating between normal tissue and pathological conditions like adenomas and adenocarcinomas.
observe_decisions.color_mode_reasoning: Since this is an RGB pathology image, color differences in the stain can provide important diagnostic information, thus using per-channel mode is beneficial.
observe_decisions.filtration_reasoning: Sublevel filtration is suitable as it captures dark-on-light structures, such as stained cells and tissue boundaries, which are prominent in H&E stained histopathology images.
